title: Transaction Sync Client
summary: Extend a small transaction sync client from one-page import to robust pagination, retry, de-dupe, and high-water mark behavior.

evidence.note: Maps to backend workflow extension, realistic financial data movement, and practical API robustness.

rubric.label: Codebase comprehension
rubric.excellent: Explains the API/store boundary and current one-page limitation before coding.

rubric.label: Problem framing
rubric.excellent: Clarifies retryable errors, cursor semantics, duplicate IDs, and partial failure behavior.

rubric.label: AI direction
rubric.excellent: Uses AI for edge cases and review while choosing the sync invariant personally.

rubric.label: Command of solution
rubric.excellent: Owns the high-water mark and idempotency design.

rubric.label: MVP judgment
rubric.excellent: Adds a small loop/retry path without building a framework.

rubric.label: Correctness
rubric.excellent: Handles pagination, duplicate transactions, retryable failures, and cursor updates.

rubric.label: Testing and validation
rubric.excellent: Validates multi-page and retry behavior with deterministic tests.

rubric.label: Tradeoff reasoning
rubric.excellent: Names production concerns like backoff, observability, and transactional persistence.

rubric.label: Communication and cadence
rubric.excellent: Keeps a clear running status of what has been proven.

checkpoints.title: Read the code
checkpoints.prompt: Summarize the API/store flow and the current limitation.

checkpoints.title: Frame ambiguity
checkpoints.prompt: What assumptions are you making about retry and cursor behavior?

checkpoints.title: MVP plan
checkpoints.prompt: State your sync invariant and test plan.

checkpoints.title: Risk check
checkpoints.prompt: What failure mode worries you most right now?

checkpoints.title: Final explanation
checkpoints.prompt: Explain what is robust, what is not production-grade yet, and how AI helped.
